elements.sent-2411-008: Following the findings of the first experiment, we fine-tune the backbone with LoRA.
elements.sent-2411-009: The results can be seen in Figure \ref{fig:res2 .}
elements.sent-2411-010: Compared to the initial performance, CLIP performs better with only one or two samples per class for the datasets with fewer classes \cite{kaggle2022bodycavity, Hussain2020, Plissiti2018sipakmed .}
elements.sent-2411-011: For the HiCervix dataset, 50 shots are required, but this still represents a small portion (x\%) of the total data.
elements.sent-2411-012: CLIP is followed in performance by ViT and QUILT.
elements.sent-2411-013: Models pre-trained or fine-tuned on medical or histopathological images require more data to reach this level of performance.
elements.sent-2411-014: This suggests that starting with a model exposed to greater data diversity during pre-training is beneficial when finetuning with a limited number of samples.
elements.sent-2411-015: Hypothesis on why fine-tuning from CLIP is better than fine-tuning from basic Vision models: -given that it has seen the most data (more iamges + added value of text-image pairings) - can we back it up with other similar observations in the literature ? Hypothesis on why fine-tuning from CLIP is better that fine-tuning from other Vision-language models: -Fine-tuned VLMs, even those that have been fine-tuned from CLIP, can lose generalisability.

elements.sent-2411-021: This work was partly funded by the MedReSyst project, supported by FEDER and the Walloon Region.
elements.sent-2411-022: The present research benefited from computational resources made available on Lucia, the Tier-1 supercomputer of the Walloon Region, infrastructure funded by the Walloon Region under the grant agreement n°1910247.. \bibliographystyle{IEEEbib.}
elements.sent-2411-023: Cytology slides are essential tools in diagnosing and staging cancer, but their analysis is time-consuming and costly.
elements.sent-2411-024: Foundation models have shown great potential to assist in these tasks.
elements.sent-2411-025: In this paper, we explore how existing foundation models can be applied to cytological classification.
elements.sent-2411-026: More particularly, we focus on low-rank adaptation, a parameter-efficient fine-tuning method suited to few-shot learning.
elements.sent-2411-027: We evaluated five foundation models across four cytological classification datasets.
elements.sent-2411-028: Our results demonstrate that fine-tuning the pre-trained backbones with LoRA significantly improves model performance compared to fine-tuning only the classifier head, achieving state-of-the-art results on both simple and complex classification tasks while requiring fewer data samples.
elements.sent-2411-029: Our source code is available on GitHub \href{https://github.com/mdausort/Cytology-fine-tuning {https://github.com/mdausort/Cytology-fine-tuning .}}
elements.sent-2411-030: This is a study for which no ethical approval was required.
elements.sent-2411-032: Godelaine are funded by the MedReSyst project, supported by FEDER and the Walloon Region.. M.
